data.title: Amerant Bancorp Highlights Strong First-Quarter 2026 Performance
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Amerant Bancorp ( (AMTB) ) is now available.

Amerant Bancorp reported that in the first quarter of 2026 it generated net income of $17.9 million, or $0.44 per diluted share, up sharply from $2.7 million, or $0.07 per diluted share, in the fourth quarter of 2025, driven by improved efficiency and portfolio optimization. The quarter saw modest growth in assets, loans and deposits, lower noninterest expense, a stronger efficiency ratio and higher returns on assets and equity, alongside continued credit risk management efforts that included reductions in classified loans but a slight rise in non-performing assets.

Amerant’s balance sheet shifts included higher investment securities and core deposits, reduced cash and cash equivalents, and a loan-to-deposit ratio that edged down to 85.07%, while net interest income and margin declined amid a lower average loan yield and steady deposit costs. The board also reinforced shareholder returns by repurchasing about 859,000 Class A shares for roughly $18.7 million and declaring a $0.09 per-share cash dividend on common stock payable on May 29, 2026, to shareholders of record on May 15, 2026.

To discuss its first-quarter 2026 performance and strategic progress, Amerant scheduled a live earnings webcast and conference call for April 24, 2026, with supporting materials available through its investor relations website, underscoring a focus on transparency with investors. Management emphasized the bank’s strong fundamentals, cost-saving initiatives and disciplined credit posture as key to supporting long-term growth, stability and value creation for customers, communities and shareholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (AMTB) stock is a Hold with a $23.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Amerant Bancorp stock, see the AMTB Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on AMTB Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, AMTB is a Neutral.

AMTB scores 56 driven primarily by an improving but still uneven financial profile and a cautious earnings-call backdrop highlighting profitability, expense, and asset-quality headwinds. Technicals are mixed with limited near-term momentum, while valuation is reasonable and modestly supportive.

To see Spark’s full report on AMTB stock, click here.

More about Amerant Bancorp

Amerant Bancorp Inc. is a bank holding company headquartered in Coral Gables, Florida, operating primarily through Amerant Bank, N.A. and Amerant Investments, Inc. The company provides deposit, credit and wealth management services to individuals and businesses, supported by a network of 23 banking centers across South Florida and Tampa, reflecting its regional focus in the Florida banking market.

Average Trading Volume: 279,394

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $947.8M

For a thorough assessment of AMTB stock, go to TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Customers Bancorp Posts Strong Q1 2026 Results, Growth
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Customers Bancorp ( (CUBI) ) has provided an update.

Customers Bancorp reported strong first-quarter 2026 results on April 23, 2026, with net income available to common shareholders of $69.7 million, or $1.97 per diluted share, and core earnings essentially in line. The bank delivered ROAA of 1.13%, ROCE of about 13%, an efficiency ratio below 50%, and year-over-year gains in revenue, core earnings, and book value per share of 58%, 28% and 16%, respectively.

Loans and leases held for investment rose 3.7% quarter-over-quarter and 15.3% year-over-year to $17.4 billion, driven by specialized C&I, mortgage finance, and commercial real estate segments, while investment securities decreased modestly. Deposits grew 3.9% from the prior quarter and 14.0% year-over-year to $21.6 billion, with a lower average cost and record non-interest-bearing balances, as capital and tangible common equity climbed to $2.1 billion and asset quality remained strong with NPAs at 0.29% of total assets and reserves at 337% of non-performing loans.

The quarter also marked the completion of a planned leadership transition effective January 1, 2026, with Sam Sidhu succeeding Jay Sidhu as CEO and emphasizing disciplined growth in structure and credit quality. The company increased borrowings mainly through FHLB advances, enhanced liquidity coverage of uninsured deposits to roughly 151%, improved its TCE/TA ratio to 8.3%, and repurchased over 621,000 common shares, signaling confidence in its franchise strength and balance sheet resilience.

The most recent analyst rating on (CUBI) stock is a Buy with a $84.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Customers Bancorp stock, see the CUBI Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CUBI Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CUBI is a Outperform.

Overall score is supported by a strong, positive earnings-call outlook and favorable price momentum above key moving averages. This is tempered by only mid-level financial statement quality driven by normalized profitability versus 2021–2022 and historical cash-flow volatility, while valuation appears reasonable on an ~11x P/E.

To see Spark’s full report on CUBI stock, click here.

More about Customers Bancorp

Customers Bancorp, Inc., based in West Reading, Pa., operates as a commercial bank holding company focused on loans and leases to commercial and industrial clients, commercial real estate borrowers, and mortgage finance customers. The bank emphasizes specialized C&I lending, multifamily and owner-occupied CRE, and maintains a significant base of non-interest-bearing and digitally sourced deposits.

Average Trading Volume: 406,678

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $2.63B

A10 Networks ( (ATEN) ) just unveiled an update.

A10 Networks, Inc. held its 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders on April 22, 2026, with 92.56% of eligible shares represented in person or by proxy. Stockholders elected five directors, including Tor R. Braham, Peter Y. Chung, Eric Singer, Dhrupad Trivedi and Dana Wolf, to serve until the 2027 annual meeting, reflecting broad support for the board slate.

Investors also approved, on a non-binding basis, the company’s executive compensation program and backed holding say-on-pay advisory votes on an annual schedule. In addition, stockholders ratified the appointment of Grant Thornton LLP as the independent registered public accounting firm for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2026, reinforcing continuity in A10 Networks’ governance and oversight structures.

data.title: ClearOne Completes Reincorporation to Nevada, Updates Governance
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Clearone Communications ( (CLRO) ) has shared an announcement.

On April 22, 2026, ClearOne, Inc. completed a legal reincorporation from Delaware to Nevada, with the change becoming effective that day at 4:00 p.m. Eastern Time. The company’s name and Nasdaq listing under ticker CLRO remain unchanged, but its governance is now subject to Nevada law, new articles of incorporation, and new bylaws approved by its board.

As part of the move, ClearOne adopted Nevada Articles authorizing 150,000,000 common and 50,000,000 preferred shares, including specified Class A Redeemable and Class B Convertible Preferred Stock. The company stated that the reincorporation did not alter its business, assets, liabilities, or operations, primarily affecting shareholder rights and corporate governance rather than day-to-day activities.

Spark’s Take on CLRO Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CLRO is a Neutral.

The score is driven down primarily by severe financial deterioration (revenue collapse, large losses, negative free cash flow, and negative equity). Technicals also point to a sustained downtrend despite oversold readings, and corporate events add near-term uncertainty due to Nasdaq compliance risk.

To see Spark’s full report on CLRO stock, click here.

More about Clearone Communications

ClearOne, Inc. is a Nasdaq-listed company in the communications technology sector, with its common stock trading on The Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol CLRO. The company’s capital structure now authorizes up to 200,000,000 shares, including 150,000,000 shares of common stock and 50,000,000 shares of preferred stock with designated Class A Redeemable and Class B Convertible series.

data.title: JPMorgan Chase Issues $10 Billion in New Debt
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from JPMorgan Chase ( (JPM) ).

On April 23, 2026, JPMorgan Chase & Co. closed a series of registered public debt offerings totaling $10 billion across multiple tranches of floating rate and fixed-to-floating rate notes maturing between 2030 and 2037. The issuance, supported by a filed legal opinion on the validity of the securities, underscores the bank’s continued access to capital markets and adds to its long-term funding profile, which is important for balance sheet management and ongoing financing activities.

By tapping investors for both shorter- and longer-dated instruments, the firm diversified its liability structure and appealed to demand for interest rate–sensitive products. This transaction may enhance JPMorgan Chase’s flexibility in managing interest rate risk and regulatory capital needs while reinforcing its role as a major issuer in the global debt markets.

The most recent analyst rating on (JPM) stock is a Buy with a $363.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on JPMorgan Chase stock, see the JPM Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on JPM Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, JPM is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by strong financial performance (revenue and profitability resilience, improving leverage) and supportive technical trend (price above major moving averages with positive MACD). The rating is tempered by weak/volatile reported cash flow, and by earnings-call risks around higher expense levels and regulatory-capital/RWA headwinds alongside elevated card credit costs; valuation is reasonable with a modest dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on JPM stock, click here.

More about JPMorgan Chase

JPMorgan Chase & Co. is a leading global financial services firm operating in banking, investment banking, asset management, and securities services. It provides lending, capital markets, advisory, and treasury solutions to corporations, governments, institutions, and consumers worldwide, with a strong presence in U.S. and international capital markets.

First Financial Bancorp ( (FFBC) ) has shared an update.

On April 23, 2026, First Financial Bancorp reported strong first-quarter 2026 results, with net income rising to $74.4 million, or $0.71 per diluted share, compared with $62.4 million, or $0.64 per share, in the prior quarter. The bank posted a robust net interest margin near 4%, record adjusted quarterly revenue and fee income, and improved returns on assets and tangible equity, supported by resilient funding costs and strong leasing, wealth management and foreign exchange income.

The quarter also reflected integration progress and growth from the Westfield Bank conversion and the January 1, 2026 closing of the BankFinancial acquisition, which boosted deposits and loans while temporarily lifting expenses. Asset quality and capital ratios remained solid, and the board authorized a new share repurchase plan of up to 5 million shares effective April 21, 2026, signaling confidence in the balance sheet and capital strength and positioning the company to deploy buybacks as part of its broader capital strategy through 2027.

On April 23, 2026, Huntington Bancshares announced that its board declared a quarterly cash dividend on its common stock of $0.155 per share, unchanged from the previous quarter, payable on July 1, 2026, to shareholders of record as of June 17. The company also approved quarterly dividends across seven preferred stock series, with most to be paid on July 15, 2026, and the Series L dividend scheduled for August 20, 2026, underscoring a continued commitment to returning capital to both common and preferred shareholders.

The preferred dividends cover its floating-rate Series B and fixed-rate reset Series F, G, H, J, K, and L issues, with record dates in early July and August depending on the series. By maintaining its common dividend level and fulfilling obligations on a wide slate of preferred securities, Huntington signaled ongoing balance-sheet stability and consistent income for income-focused investors in its capital structure.

data.title: Ameris Bancorp Posts Strong First-Quarter 2026 Financial Results
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ameris Bancorp ( (ABCB) ) has shared an announcement.

Ameris Bancorp reported a strong first quarter of 2026, with net income rising to $110.5 million, or $1.63 per diluted share, for the period ended March 31, 2026, up from $87.9 million a year earlier, driven by higher net interest income and robust noninterest revenue. Return on average assets reached 1.62%, return on average tangible common equity climbed to 14.75% and net interest margin expanded to 3.88%, underscoring outperformance versus much of the regional banking sector.

Earning assets grew by $607.8 million on an annualized basis, supported by $314.5 million in loan growth and $260.7 million in deposit growth, while the noninterest-bearing deposit mix improved to 29.8% of total deposits, helping reduce the total cost of funds to 1.88%. The efficiency ratio improved year over year to 49.97% despite seasonal expense pressures, credit quality remained solid with annualized net charge-offs of 0.21% and stable reserves and the company returned capital by repurchasing about $74.9 million of stock, or roughly 1.4% of outstanding equity, while growing tangible book value per share to $44.79.

Noninterest income increased 13.1% from the prior quarter to $69.9 million, with mortgage banking revenue up 16.1% and other fee income helped by the absence of prior mortgage servicing right losses and higher gains on SBA loan sales. Total assets reached $28.11 billion, shareholders’ equity stood at $4.08 billion and tangible common equity represented 11.15% of tangible assets, positioning Ameris with a stronger balance sheet and capital base as it continues to expand in its Southeast markets.

The most recent analyst rating on (ABCB) stock is a Hold with a $85.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Ameris Bancorp stock, see the ABCB Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on ABCB Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, ABCB is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by solid fundamentals (profitability and a strong, conservatively levered balance sheet), partially offset by cash flow volatility. Earnings-call guidance and sentiment were constructive, while technicals are mixed and valuation is reasonable but not especially supported by yield.

To see Spark’s full report on ABCB stock, click here.

More about Ameris Bancorp

Ameris Bancorp is a regional financial services company and bank holding company for Ameris Bank, operating primarily across attractive markets in the U.S. Southeast. The company offers a range of banking products, including commercial and consumer loans, deposit accounts and treasury services, as well as a sizable retail mortgage business and SBA lending platform that contribute meaningfully to fee income and balance-sheet growth.

The bank focuses on profitable franchise expansion in its core Southeast footprint, emphasizing a strong mix of noninterest-bearing deposits and disciplined credit underwriting. Management highlights operating efficiency and net interest margin performance as key differentiators versus peers, supporting returns on tangible common equity that outpace much of the broader regional banking industry.

Average Trading Volume: 572,571

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $5.77B

data.title: IQVIA Shareholders Approve New 2026 Incentive Plan
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from IQVIA Holdings ( (IQV) ).

On April 23, 2026, IQVIA Holdings Inc. stockholders approved a new 2026 Incentive and Stock Award Plan at the company’s annual meeting, replacing the 2017 plan and authorizing a broad mix of performance- and time-based equity awards, including options, stock appreciation rights, restricted stock and units. The Leadership Development and Compensation Committee also adopted new standard award agreements, with performance shares tied to metrics such as revenue, adjusted EBITDA, earnings per share, cash flow and relative total shareholder return, reinforcing IQVIA’s equity-based pay framework for employees and executives.

At the same meeting, shareholders elected all board nominees to one-year terms and gave advisory approval to 2025 executive compensation, while ratifying PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP as independent auditor for 2026. Investors, however, rejected a stockholder proposal to separate the chairman and CEO roles, indicating continued support for IQVIA’s current governance structure and leadership model.

The most recent analyst rating on (IQV) stock is a Buy with a $221.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on IQVIA Holdings stock, see the IQV Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on IQV Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, IQV is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by decent underlying fundamentals but constrained by high leverage and recent margin/cash-flow pressure. Technicals are notably weak (below major moving averages with bearish momentum), while valuation is only moderate. The latest earnings call was constructive with solid FY2026 guidance and strong backlog, partially offset by margin pressure and rising interest expense.

To see Spark’s full report on IQV stock, click here.

IQVIA Holdings Inc. is a global provider of advanced analytics, technology solutions and clinical research services to the life sciences industry. The company focuses on supporting pharmaceutical, biotech and healthcare clients with data-driven tools and services to optimize drug development, commercialization and real-world evidence generation across global markets.

data.title: OceanFirst Financial Posts Solid Q1 2026 Earnings Improvement
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Oceanfirst Financial ( (OCFC) ) has shared an announcement.

OceanFirst Financial Corp. reported first-quarter 2026 net income available to common stockholders of $20.5 million, or $0.36 per diluted share, flat versus a year earlier but up from $13.1 million, or $0.23, in the fourth quarter of 2025. Core earnings rose to $24.3 million, or $0.43 per diluted share, while core pre-tax, pre-provision earnings increased to $34.4 million, reflecting improved returns on assets and tangible equity.

The quarter, which ended March 31, 2026, was marked by a six-basis-point expansion in net interest margin to 2.93%, $1.2 million growth in net interest income to $96.4 million, and a 13% reduction in non-interest expense to $73.4 million. Total loans grew $91.9 million at a 3% annualized rate, led by robust commercial and industrial lending, as management highlighted strong results from loan growth, margin expansion, and cost control while progressing toward a pending merger with Flushing Financial Corporation, which has secured key approvals but is still awaiting final regulatory clearance.

The most recent analyst rating on (OCFC) stock is a Hold with a $20.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Oceanfirst Financial stock, see the OCFC Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on OCFC Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, OCFC is a Neutral.

OCFC scores in the low-to-mid 60s primarily due to moderate but weakening financial performance (revenue contraction, margin/ROE compression, higher leverage). Offsetting this, technicals are modestly bullish (trading above key moving averages with supportive RSI/MACD) and valuation is supported by a reasonable P/E and strong dividend yield. The latest earnings call adds a constructive tilt via reiterated growth and NIM-improvement guidance, tempered by near-term margin/noninterest income headwinds and one-time costs.

To see Spark’s full report on OCFC stock, click here.

More about Oceanfirst Financial

OceanFirst Financial Corp., headquartered in Red Bank, New Jersey, is the holding company for OceanFirst Bank N.A., a regional banking institution listed on NASDAQ under the ticker OCFC. The bank focuses on traditional commercial and consumer lending, including commercial and industrial loans, and competes in a margin-sensitive market where efficiency and disciplined expense management are key to profitability.
